$comment: Example MCP host config for the kbexplorer affordance server (kbx mcp, PE3-F4). This is the OPTIONAL, non-canvas delivery path: point a plain `copilot -p` run (or any MCP client) at this to expose the kbexplorer affordances as MCP tools named kbx_<affordance>. If you use the canvas extension instead, you do NOT need this — the tools ship in-process with the canvas and no MCP config is consulted. BYO-cred: the server inherits your ambient environment (gh, provider keys).

kbexplorer.$comment-allow: Add "--allow" to args (or set env KBX_MCP_CONSENT=allow) to auto-approve write/sample actions in trusted automation. Without it, those actions request consent via MCP elicitation and fail closed when the client cannot elicit.
